### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-21
Well, I have gotten it to work sort of. I can get the stylus to work and pressure sensitivity in GIMP. But, I can't get the pen button or the eraser to work. The cursor doesn't follow the pen when I turn it to the eraser side. Here's my xorg.conf:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"

	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"i2c"

	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
	Option		"LeftEdge"		"1700"
	Option		"RightEdge"		"5300"
	Option		"TopEdge"		"1700"
	Option		"BottomEdge"		"4200"
	Option		"FingerLo"		"25"
	Option		"FingerHigh"		"30"
	Option		"MaxTapTime"		"180"
	Option		"MaxTapMove"		"220"
	Option		"VertScrollDelta"	"100"
	Option		"MinSpeed"		"0.09"
	Option		"MaxSpeed"		"0.18"
	Option		"AccelFactor"		"0.0015"
	Option		"PalmMinWidth"		"70"
	Option		"PalmMinZ"		"200"
	Option		"SHMConfig"		"on"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Trident Microsystems CyberBlade XPAi1"
	Driver		"trident"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Trident Microsystems CyberBlade XPAi1"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768"

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"

	Mode	0666

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"

  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom0"
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

```

Any suggestions?

### Post by 23meg on 2006-01-21
Here are my relevant sections```
Section "InputDevice"
    Driver        "wacom"
    Identifier    "cursor"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/ttyS14"
    Option        "InputFashion" "Tablet"
    Option        "ForceDevice"    "ISDV4"
    Option        "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS (SERIAL)"
    Option        "SendCoreEvents" "on"
    Option        "Type"          "cursor"
    Option        "Mode"          "absolute" 
    Option        "Vendor" "WACOM"
    Option        "Speed"         "3.0"
    Option        "Threshold"     "1"
    Option        "Tilt"          "on"
    Option	     "Button2"	"3"
    #Option    "DebugLevel"    "10"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Driver        "wacom"
    Identifier    "stylus"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/ttyS14"
    Option        "Type"          "stylus"
    Option        "InputFashion" "Pen"
    Option        "SendCoreEvents" "on"
    Option        "Name"          "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS Stylus (SERIAL)"
    Option        "ForceDevice"    "ISDV4"
    Option        "Mode"          "absolute"
    Option        "Vendor" "WACOM"
    Option        "Tilt"          "on"
    Option        "TiltInvert"    "on"
    Option        "Threshold"     "1"
    Option	     "Button2"	"3"
   #Option    "DebugLevel"    "10"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Driver       "wacom"
 Identifier   "eraser"
 Option       "Device" "/dev/ttyS14"
 Option       "InputFashion" "Eraser"
 Option       "Name" "GRAPHIRE / INTUOS Eraser (SERIAL)"
 Option       "Type" "eraser"
 Option       "Mode" "absolute"
 Option       "Vendor" "WACOM"
 Option       "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" 
 Option        "SendCoreEvents" "on"
EndSection

```

### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-21
Hmm... What do you have in your ServerLayout section? Even using your exact settings above, the eraser still doesn't function. I get no cursor movement when the pen is eraser side down.

---

### Post by 23meg on 2006-01-21
Don't use the exact settings, keep your own "Device" line. Just add the "cursor" section and add the "Button" lines.```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"ALPS"
        InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-21
Wacdump times out when it tries to connect to the tablet apparently. I've tried:

wacdump /dev/wacom0
wacdump /dev/ttyS14

Neither seems to work. I'm running it after killing X with Ctrl-Alt-Bkspc and then sudo killall gdm. Am I missing something?

---

### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-21
Well, I commented out the pen stuff from xorg.conf (as per Linux Wacom Project instructions). Still timing out. Here's what I'm getting:

damonkohler@wumpus:~$ sudo wacdump -v /dev/wacom0
16:17:17.880 INFO: Opening log
16:17:17.881 INFO: Setting baud rate to 38400
16:17:17.881 INFO: Sending reset
16:17:17.210 INFO: Setting baud rate to 19200
16:17:17.210 INFO: Sending reset
16:17:17.539 INFO: Setting baud rate to 9600
16:17:17.539 INFO: Sending reset
WacomOpenTablet: Connection timed out
damonkohler@wumpus:~$

However, if I move the pen around while it's doing that, I get some warnings about stuff being discarded. That doesn't happen if I'm moving the eraser.

---

### Post by 23meg on 2006-01-21
Try this:```
wacdump -f c100 /dev/ttyS14
```

---

### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-22
Yeah, that works. The eraser only shows up some times. Most of the time, it doesn't register. Stylus shows up consistently and the button works. It's a brand new digitizer.... Is it possible that the pen itself is broken? If wacdump isn't registering the eraser, that makes me think there is a hardware problem.

---

### Post by 23meg on 2006-01-23
[QUOTE=damonkohler]  If wacdump isn't registering the eraser, that makes me think there is a hardware problem.[/QUOTE]Me too, since it outputs raw data from the device. Can you check if the eraser works consistently in Windows?

---

### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-26
Well, two digitizers later, my pen AND my eraser seem to be working. Now, I just can't seem to erase anything in GIMP. Will have a look around for the answer but wouldn't mind a quick fix suggestion :)

---

### Post by damonkohler on 2006-01-27
Ok, everything works now. Setting the debug level of the eraser to 5 and pressing it against the screen indicated that it was sending a mouse button 3 click. So, I just added:

Option "Button3" "1"

To the XInput section for it and everything works now.

Thanks for the help.

---
